name: Snipe-IT
what: Open-source IT asset management tool for hardware, accessories, consumables, licenses, and user/device assignment.
solves: Internal asset inventory, check-in/check-out, depreciation, and auditability for organizational equipment.
users: IT and operations teams managing internal equipment fleets.
fit: Limited for customer-facing rentals. Useful if Hylono mainly wants internal device/office asset control, but Leihs is a better fit for rental/lending workflows.
runtime: Laravel/PHP web app with database, mail, queues/scheduled jobs, and file uploads.
components: Web UI/API, relational database, mailer, cron/jobs, storage, and auth integrations like LDAP/SSO options.
deploy: Commonly self-hosted via Docker or standard PHP stack behind a reverse proxy.
deps: Database, PHP runtime, mailer, storage, cron/scheduler, and optional LDAP/AD integrations.
auth: Local accounts plus optional LDAP/AD; review SSO options if needed.
apis: REST API, import/export, reports, and asset/audit workflows.
security: Contains inventory records, user assignments, and audit data. Standard web-app hardening and backup controls are required.
setup_path: Follow official install docs, pick a supported DB/runtime combination, and verify whether internal IT asset scope justifies separate deployment from rental tooling.
env: APP_URL, DB settings, mailer config, app key, cron, storage path, and LDAP settings if used.
persistence: Back up database and uploaded asset files.
operations: Laravel/PHP upgrades, cron reliability, import quality, and access-role review.
failure_points: Trying to bend it into a customer rental system, or letting asset ownership drift between Snipe-IT and other systems.
integration: Best used only for internal Hylono equipment if needed. Limited direct value to the public site beyond admin links or reporting extracts.
verify: Clarify whether Hylono needs internal IT asset management separately from customer rental/loan workflows.
risk: Low to moderate if scoped internally; poor fit if stretched into rental operations.
gdpr: Mostly operational asset data, but assigned-user records still count as personal data.
overlap: Leihs covers lending/rental better; Snipe-IT is for internal asset control.
codex_read_first: Read Leihs and Snipe-IT packages together before choosing an asset/rental platform.
codex_best_path: Use Snipe-IT only if internal IT asset management is a distinct need separate from customer equipment lending.
codex_never_assume: Never assume Snipe-IT is the right backbone for customer-facing rentals.
